title: lygodesmia exigua
intro: Lygodesmia exigua, also known as the small skeleton plant or desert skeleton plant, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. Lygodesmia exigua has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The plant's root and leaves have been used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which are believed to have health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving digestive issues. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been studied for their potential antimicrobial and antioxidant properties.
